mnemonics: Mean, Median, Mode: My Magical Mom
Probability = Number of Favorable Outcomes / Total Possible Outcomes
keyPoints: The probability distribution is a function that describes the probability of each possible outcome in a random experiment.
The sum of the probabilities of all possible outcomes is equal to 1.
Expected value (mean) is the sum of each value multiplied by its probability.
Variance is the average of the squared differences from the mean.
Standard Deviation is the square root of the variance.
